summary: Verified the new secure change-password + admin-only reset-link flow on the live Atlas deployment. Full backend regression suite passes (87/87: 81 passed + 6 skipped, 0 failures, 0 errors). All 9 contract tests in test_password_flows.py PASSED. Frontend: /forgot-password renders + submits + surfaces rate-limit toast, /reset-password?token=bogus shows the 'Link cannot be used' error block on mount, AdminLogin shows 'Forgot password?' link, AdminLayout Change-password button opens ChangePasswordModal with fields Current/New/Confirm + policy hint ('Min 8 characters · must include a number or special character'), UserManagement Reset-password icon (KeyRound) opens ResetLinkModal displaying full-origin https://casting-deck-pro.preview.emergentagent.com/reset-password?token=... URL, Copy-link button, target email, 1-hour TTL, and 'We never store the raw token' confirmation. 401 auth-page guard verified: forgot/reset pages do NOT redirect to /admin/login when backend returns error responses.
action_items: Cleanup: the testing run generated ONE admin-scoped reset-password token (clicking the Reset button for admin@talentgram.com in UserManagement). It was NOT consumed, so Admin@123 is still the active password, but the hashed token sits in password_reset_tokens until its 1-hour TTL prunes it. If paranoid, issue a fresh reset for the same admin (the backend's delete_many({user_id, used_at:None}) will drop the stale hash) or wait for the TTL index to auto-expire.
critical_code_review_comments: routers/password.py _check_rate_limit: bucket key is client IP from x-forwarded-for (first hop). Behind shared ingress / NAT this buckets multiple users into the same IP — legitimate users may see 429 after other users exhaust the 5/15min quota. Acceptable trade-off given task spec, but documenting it.
password.py is a single module with 4 endpoints + rate-limit helpers + lookup helper — 210 lines, nicely under the 700-line guideline. Good split from users.py.

core.py enforce_password_policy is lenient (digit OR symbol — not both). Matches the stated spec. Suggest documenting in the modal hint that diacritics/unicode 'symbols' count (current impl uses `not c.isalnum()` which includes spaces — arguably space-only passwords with 8 chars pass the policy).

admin_generate_reset_link (users.py L169-204) stores email on the token doc — this is convenient but couples user PII into the reset table. The user_id lookup already gives us the email on consume; consider dropping the denormalised email field to reduce the blast radius if the collection ever leaks.

No audit log for reset-link generation. For a security feature it would be valuable to push a row into an audit_log collection (admin_id, target_id, created_at, ip) — today only an INFO log line is emitted.
_lookup_reset_token raises 'invalid or has already been used' for missing docs and 'has already been used' for consumed docs — good, both responses are generic enough to not leak token validity.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on critical paths (forgot + reset + change + admin reset-link flows all verified)

test_credentials: admin@talentgram.com / Admin@123 — confirmed working. Admin password was NOT modified.
seed_data_creation: test_password_flows.py invites + activates a throwaway team user (pwtest+<hex>@example.com) per test and tears it down via DELETE /api/users/{uid}. One admin reset-password token was created via the UI test and left to auto-expire.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/ -q --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/out.xml` — Atlas latency makes the full suite take ~3-4 min. The /forgot-password rate limiter is process-local and shared across tests — expect 429 on back-to-back runs; tests already tolerate 200|429. The 401 interceptor in /app/frontend/src/lib/api.js is auth-page-guarded (/admin/login, /forgot-password, /reset-password, /signup) — when adding new public auth pages, extend that whitelist or the page will infinite-redirect on a 401. UserManagement reset button is an icon-only KeyRound button with data-testid='reset-pw-btn-{uid}' and title='Reset password' — selectors using visible text won't work.
